message:
chore(beads): close offline-preflight issue, add agent-mail sqlite corruption tracker

- Close flywheel_connectors-5w1wb ("[fwc] offline preflight should
  succeed with offline-artifact exit semantics") — resolved in prior
  commit f664374c
- Add flywheel_connectors-d5yeb ("[tooling] MCP Agent Mail storage.sqlite3
  corruption blocks registration and inbox access") tracking the
  storage.sqlite3 corruption issue affecting agent mail registration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","title":"TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests (Introspection, Tool Descriptors, Discovery)","description":"# TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests

## Goal
Verify that FCP2 operations are discoverable and safe for AI agents.

## Test Categories

### 1. Introspection Schema Tests
- Introspection object schema validation (canonical CBOR)
- All required fields present (operations, schemas, risk levels)
- Golden vectors for introspection responses

### 2. Operation Discovery Tests
- Enumerate all connector operations
- Each operation has input/output schemas
- Risk tier correctly reported
- Rate limits correctly reported

### 3. MCP Tool Descriptor Tests
- Mapping to MCP-compatible tool descriptors
- Tool name, description, examples present
- Input/output schema translation
- Risk tier to MCP safety level mapping

### 4. E2E Discovery Tests
- Agent can enumerate tools from connector
- Agent can invoke safe operation end-to-end
- Agent can read recovery hints on failure

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON with operation_id, schema versions
- Discovery timing metrics
- Error details with recovery hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- Introspection is complete for agent operation
- No hardcoded connector knowledge required
- Tool descriptors match MCP conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:50:32.192813258Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312640984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312513066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent","fcp2","mvp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","title":"TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-telemetry` emits spec-required metrics/logging fields, preserves correlation, and never leaks secrets.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for metrics, logging, and tracing library.

## Test Categories

### Structured Logging
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] JSON output format
- [ ] Field injection (correlation IDs, zone, connector)
- [ ] Sensitive data redaction
- [ ] Log rotation handling

### Metrics Collection
- [ ] Counter increments
- [ ] Gauge values
- [ ] Histogram distributions
- [ ] Timing measurements
- [ ] Label/tag handling

### Distributed Tracing
- [ ] Span creation and nesting
- [ ] Context propagation
- [ ] Trace ID generation
- [ ] Span attributes
- [ ] Sampling decisions

### Export
- [ ] Prometheus format export
- [ ] OTLP format export
- [ ] Custom sink support
- [ ] Batch flushing

### Performance
- [ ] Low overhead in hot path
- [ ] Async export
- [ ] Buffer management

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5vy","title":"fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway","description":"# Inbound Event Gateway Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Universal webhook receiver for external service integrations.

## Overview

Many services push events via webhooks (GitHub, Stripe, Discord, Slack, etc.). Instead of building separate webhook handlers for each service, this meta-connector provides a unified HTTP endpoint infrastructure with per-service configuration.

## Core Components

### 1. HTTP Endpoint Management
- Dynamic endpoint creation/deletion
- Unique URL per webhook source
- Support for path-based routing (`/webhooks/{service}/{id}`)
- Health check endpoints
- CORS and preflight handling
- Rate limiting per endpoint

### 2. Signature Verification (Per-Service)
- Pluggable verification strategies:
  - HMAC-SHA256 (GitHub, Stripe)
  - HMAC-SHA1 (legacy services)
  - Ed25519 (Discord)
  - API key header
  - Basic auth
  - Custom verification functions
- Timing-safe comparison
- Signature caching for replay prevention
- Failed verification alerting

### 3. Payload Transformation
- JSON schema validation
- Payload normalization to common event format
- Field mapping/extraction
- Nested payload unwrapping
- Binary payload handling (multipart)
- Compression support (gzip, deflate)

### 4. Event Routing to Zones
- Route events to appropriate FCP zones
- Support multiple destinations per event type
- Filter events based on payload content
- Priority-based routing
- Fan-out to multiple handlers

### 5. Retry/Dead-Letter Handling
- Configurable retry strategies:
  - Exponential backoff
  - Fixed interval
  - Fibonacci backoff
- Dead letter queue for failed events
- Manual retry interface
- Event replay capability
- TTL-based expiration

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[webhooks]]
name = "github-repo-events"
path = "/webhooks/github/main-repo"
signature_type = "hmac-sha256"
signature_header = "X-Hub-Signature-256"
secret = "${GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.github-automation"
event_type_header = "X-GitHub-Event"

[[webhooks]]
name = "stripe-payments"
path = "/webhooks/stripe/payments"
signature_type = "stripe"
secret = "${STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.billing"
retry_max = 5
retry_backoff = "exponential"

[[webhooks]]
name = "discord-interactions"
path = "/webhooks/discord/bot"
signature_type = "ed25519"
public_key = "${DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY}"
route_to = "zone.discord-bot"
```

## Event Format (Normalized)

```json
{
  "id": "evt_abc123",
  "source": "github",
  "type": "push",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "webhook_id": "github-repo-events",
  "payload": { ... },
  "metadata": {
    "delivery_id": "xyz789",
    "signature_valid": true,
    "received_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.123Z"
  }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Support 10+ common webhook signature types
- [ ] Sub-50ms processing latency
- [ ] 99.9% uptime for webhook endpoints
- [ ] Complete audit trail for all received webhooks
- [ ] Zero data loss with dead-letter queue
- [ ] Hot-reload of webhook configurations

## Dependencies

- HTTP server framework (axum/actix-web)
- Persistent queue for dead-letter handling
- Zone routing system

## Security Considerations

- All secrets stored encrypted
- IP allowlisting per webhook (optional)
- Request size limits
- Timeout handling
- DDoS protection integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:23:06.647234418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-3md (fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-5w1wb","title":"[fwc] offline preflight should succeed with offline-artifact exit semantics","description":"fwc preflight has an explicit offline-artifact analysis path in crates/fwc/src/main.rs, but preflight_dispatch_without_host currently returns CliExitCode::Transport even when it successfully emits a structured offline analysis payload with CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact. This makes the hybrid degraded path look like a connectivity failure to agents and scripts despite delivering useful output. Acceptance: offline fwc preflight returns success semantics with preserved offline-artifact provenance, and a regression test locks the behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:37:59.574831Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:38:17.702494Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","preflight","truthfulness"]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-5w1wb","title":"[fwc] offline preflight should succeed with offline-artifact exit semantics","description":"fwc preflight has an explicit offline-artifact analysis path in crates/fwc/src/main.rs, but preflight_dispatch_without_host currently returns CliExitCode::Transport even when it successfully emits a structured offline analysis payload with CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact. This makes the hybrid degraded path look like a connectivity failure to agents and scripts despite delivering useful output. Acceptance: offline fwc preflight returns success semantics with preserved offline-artifact provenance, and a regression test locks the behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:37:59.574831Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:54:20.192895Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:53:39.245501Z","close_reason":"Already satisfied in current tree by commit f664374c; offline preflight returns success semantics with offline-artifact provenance and existing regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","preflight","truthfulness"],"comments":[{"id":2045,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5w1wb","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Fresh pass on 2026-03-27: this bead is already satisfied in the current tree. preflight_dispatch_without_host in crates/fwc/src/main.rs now returns CliExitCode::Success and injects CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact, matching the bead acceptance text. Source history shows commit f664374c fixing this path, and the unit test preflight_offline_shows_risk_and_approval_info already asserts success status plus offline-artifact provenance. I attempted targeted rch exec verification, but rch 1.0.16 hit dependency-preflight RCH-E326 and tried to fail open to local Cargo, which I aborted per repo rules. Closing this bead as stale or already-fixed state. Agent Mail registration remains blocked by the corrupted storage.sqlite3 database refusing automatic recovery while WAL/SHM sidecars are live.","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:54:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5y7","title":"fcp-config: Configuration Validation and Schema Framework","description":"# fcp-config: Configuration Validation and Schema Framework

## Overview
Type-safe configuration framework with JSON Schema validation, secret handling, and environment variable support for FCP connectors.

## Schema Validation
- JSON Schema Draft 7 validation
- Required field enforcement
- Type validation (string, number, bool, array, object)
- Format validation (uri, email, uuid, datetime)
- Custom validators
- Nested schema support

## Configuration Loading
- TOML file parsing
- JSON file parsing
- Environment variable expansion (${VAR})
- File path resolution
- Default value injection
- Configuration merging

## Secret Handling
- Secret placeholder syntax: ${secret:name}
- Environment variable secrets
- File-based secrets (Docker secrets)
- Vault integration (future)
- No secrets in logs/errors

## Validation Errors
- Human-readable error messages
- JSON path to invalid field
- Expected vs actual type
- Suggested fixes
- Multiple error aggregation

## Configuration Schema Definition
- Derive macros for Rust structs
- Automatic schema generation
- Documentation extraction

## Features
- Hot reload support
- Change detection
- Validation caching
- Cross-field validation

## Dependencies
- serde, serde_json, toml
- jsonschema, regex
- fcp-core","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:49:58.772040571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:33.857911504Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:33.857911504Z","close_reason":"Superseded by FCP2 Connector Manifest (1n78.19). Configuration validation is now part of manifest parsing/verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5yw","title":"fcp.figma: Component extraction and inspection","description":"# fcp.figma: Component extraction and inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Figma component metadata so agents can:
- identify reusable components and variants
- inspect component properties (auto-layout, constraints)
- support design-to-code workflows

## Scope
- List components and component sets for a file or library.
- Fetch component details:
  - name
  - key/id
  - description
  - variant properties (for sets)
- Find component instances within a file (where supported).

## Performance constraints
- Figma files can be very large; component discovery must be:
  - paginated
  - bounded memory
  - avoid downloading full node trees unless explicitly requested

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.components.read`
- `figma.files.read` (for node references)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat component names/descriptions as tainted.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.component.list`
  - `figma.component.get`
  - `figma.component_set.list`
  - `figma.component.instances`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - listing pagination
  - large-file behavior (bounded)
  - error mapping (missing component, permission)

## Acceptance criteria
- Component discovery is reliable and does not require full-file downloads by default.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d4j2","title":"[IDEA-07.3] Formatting fallback tests + example wiring","description":"## Goal
Provide tests and example wiring in a connector send_message path.

## Details
- Add integration test with a fake API returning parse errors.
- Example wiring notes in connector template or docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Example shows how to hook formatter and error classifier together.
- Tests confirm fallback behavior without leaking content in logs.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d5h","title":"fcp.datadog: Dashboard access and widget rendering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:16:37.975697485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T17:10:13.135733516Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T17:10:13.135733516Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: All Datadog sub-features merged into comprehensive parent connector bead flywheel_connectors-cu3 (fcp.datadog: Datadog Observability Platform Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","title":"[tooling] MCP Agent Mail storage.sqlite3 corruption blocks registration and inbox access","description":"Agent Mail use in /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors is currently blocked before registration or inbox access. MCP tool calls such as macro_start_session, list_agents, ensure_project, and resource reads return DATABASE_ERROR with detail: refusing automatic sqlite recovery for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 while live WAL/SHM sidecars are present; stop the server and run explicit repair. Direct sqlite integrity_check confirms main DB corruption (freelist corruption, duplicate page references, out-of-order rowid, and many never-used pages). Current on-disk state includes storage.sqlite3, storage.sqlite3-wal, storage.sqlite3-shm, and storage.sqlite3.bak. This prevents the user-requested Agent Mail registration, inbox processing, and introductions to other agents. Acceptance: 1) Agent Mail can register/list agents/fetch inbox again in this repo, 2) repair is done with an explicit, auditable procedure that does not silently destroy state, 3) coordination notes capture that repair may require stopping the live MCP server and handling WAL/SHM explicitly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CobaltHarbor","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:55:37.245489Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:56:13.891704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-mail","coordination","sqlite","tooling"],"comments":[{"id":2046,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Non-destructive diagnosis on 2026-03-27: MCP Agent Mail health is green enough to answer health checks, but project-scoped operations fail with DATABASE_ERROR because /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 is corrupt and live sidecars are present. Evidence collected: read_mcp_resource on resource://projects returns error_detail saying automatic sqlite recovery is refused while WAL/SHM are live; sqlite3 PRAGMA integrity_check reports freelist corruption, duplicate page references, and rowid/order damage; ls shows storage.sqlite3 plus storage.sqlite3-wal and storage.sqlite3-shm; lsof shows Agent Mail listener processes on port 8765 including /Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/am serve-http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765 --no-tui. sqlite3 .recover emits a plausible SQL dump, so an explicit stop-and-recover procedure looks possible, but I am not running it without user approval because it would rewrite coordination state.","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:56:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d6d","title":"fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection","description":"# fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable deep triage by fetching **event-level details** for an issue, including stack traces and relevant context, while maintaining strict secret/PII hygiene.

## Scope
### Read operations
- Fetch latest event for an issue (or a specific event id).
- Retrieve:
  - stack trace frames (module/function/line)
  - breadcrumbs
  - tags
  - environment + release
  - request/user context (PII: must be handled carefully)
- Optional: retrieve attachments/artifacts metadata (download is separate + bounded).

### Output shaping
- Return a **sanitized, structured** view that is useful for debugging:
  - include stable identifiers
  - include stack traces (code locations are ok)
  - redact known secret-like fields

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.events.read`
- `sentry.artifacts.read` (if attachments are exposed)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Event payloads are untrusted and may contain secrets/PII.
  - Do not log raw payloads.
  - Apply redaction before returning “rendered” textual summaries.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.event.get`
  - `sentry.event.latest_for_issue`
  - `sentry.event.stacktrace`
- Redaction strategy:
  - allowlist safe fields
  - redact common secret patterns
  - cap maximum returned payload size

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - stack trace parsing and normalization
  - redaction correctness (golden vectors)
  - size limits + early abort
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Returns are useful for debugging without leaking secrets/PII.
- Logs remain metadata-only with correlation IDs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dcgw","title":"fcp.grafana: Open Source Observability Platform Connector","description":"# fcp.grafana - Open Source Observability Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 83/100** — Important Tier 2 connector (Observability family).

**Why Critical**: Grafana is the **leading open-source observability platform**, providing unified dashboards across metrics, logs, and traces. For organizations that cannot use Datadog (cost, self-hosted requirements, open-source preference), Grafana is the alternative. Essential for complete observability coverage.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Open Source Alternative)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge,    // Dashboard, metric queries
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Alert management
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Knowledge**: Query dashboards, metrics, logs via data sources
- **Operational**: Alert rule management, annotation CRUD
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for alerts

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Query operations are independent
- Dashboard state managed by Grafana
- No sync cursors needed

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API tokens
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Grafana Cloud
    host_allow: vec\![
        "*.grafana.net".into(),
        "grafana.com".into(),
    ],
    // Self-hosted: explicitly configured hostname
    port_allow: vec\![443, 3000],  // 3000 for self-hosted
    deny_localhost: true,   // Override for self-hosted
    deny_private_ranges: true,  // Override for self-hosted
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Telemetry data is internal/trusted
metric_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::NONE;

// Annotations may contain user input
annotation.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `grafana.dashboards.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `grafana.datasources.query` | Safe | Read-only queries |
| `grafana.alerts.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `grafana.annotations.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `grafana.annotations.write` | Risky | Creates markers |
| `grafana.dashboards.write` | Risky | Dashboard changes |
| `grafana.alerts.manage` | Dangerous | Alert configuration |
| `grafana.datasources.manage` | Dangerous | Data source config |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Dashboard & Visualization
- Query dashboard definitions
- Access panel data
- Read annotations
- Export dashboard JSON

### Data Source Queries
- Execute queries against connected data sources
- Prometheus metrics
- Loki logs
- Tempo traces

### Alerting
- List and manage alert rules
- View alert history
- Configure notification channels

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Show me the CPU dashboard for production"
"Query error rate from Prometheus"
"Find logs from the API service"
"List all triggered alerts"
"Add an annotation for this deployment"
"Export this dashboard as JSON"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Datadog Comparison
**Observability alternatives:**
- Grafana for open-source/self-hosted
- Datadog for fully managed
- Both needed for market coverage

### Prometheus/Loki Integration
- Native data source support
- Metric and log queries

### Sentry Integration
- Error rate visualization
- Alert correlation

### GitHub Integration
- Deploy annotations
- Release correlation

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **HTTP API**: Dashboard, alerts, data sources
- **Data Source Proxy**: Query through Grafana

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
| Mode | Use Case |
|------|----------|
| API Key | Service account |
| Service Account Token | Cloud |
| Basic Auth | Self-hosted (legacy) |

### Data Sources
- Prometheus
- Loki
- Tempo
- InfluxDB
- Elasticsearch
- Many more...

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:grafana:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:ops:grafana:read` | Read-only | Queries and dashboards |
| `z:ops:grafana:alerts` | Alerts only | Alert management |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Dashboard and query access
- `operational` — Alert management
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `*.grafana.net:443` (Cloud)
- Configured hostname (self-hosted)

### Credential Injection
- API tokens via egress proxy
- Service account tokens via CredentialId

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `grafana.dashboards.read` — Dashboard definitions
- `grafana.datasources.query` — Execute queries
- `grafana.alerts.read` — Alert rules
- `grafana.annotations.read` — Annotations

### Write Operations
- `grafana.dashboards.write` — Create/update dashboards
- `grafana.annotations.write` — Create annotations

### Admin Operations
- `grafana.alerts.manage` — Alert rule CRUD (Dangerous)
- `grafana.datasources.manage` — Data source config (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Get Dashboard
```
GET /api/dashboards/uid/{uid}
```

### Query Data Source
```json
POST /api/ds/query
{
  "queries": [{
    "refId": "A",
    "datasourceId": 1,
    "expr": "rate(http_requests_total[5m])"
  }],
  "from": "now-1h",
  "to": "now"
}
```

### List Alerts
```
GET /api/alertmanager/grafana/api/v2/alerts
```

### Create Annotation
```json
POST /api/annotations
{
  "dashboardUID": "abc123",
  "time": 1609459200000,
  "text": "Deployment v1.2.3"
}
```

### Search Dashboards
```
GET /api/search?query=production
```

---

## Data Source Query Examples

### Prometheus
```
rate(http_requests_total{status="500"}[5m])
```

### Loki
```
{app="api"} |= "error"
```

### Tempo
```
{resource.service.name="checkout"} && status=error
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction
- Response parsing
- Query formatting per data source
- Error handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Grafana API responses
- Multi-data-source scenarios
- Dashboard export/import

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Grafana API calls in unit tests
- Dashboard read works
- Data source queries work
- Alert management works
- Annotation CRUD works
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements
